mnemonics.title: SCAN Copy Cat
mnemonics.content: SCAN - Strcpy Copies Array iNto another. Use strcpy(dest, src) to copy source to destination

mnemonics.title: LENS
mnemonics.content: LENS - Length of ENd S. Use strlen() to count characters excluding null terminator

mnemonics.title: CATenation
mnemonics.content: CAT - ConATenate. Use strcat(dest, src) to append source string to destination

mnemonics.title: CMP
mnemonics.content: CMP - ComPare strings. strcmp() returns 0 if equal, <0 if first<string, >0 if first>string

mnemonics.title: REVerse
mnemonics.content: REV - REVerse string. Use strrev() to flip string backward

mnemonics.title: UPPer/LowER
mnemonics.content: UPR/LWR - Use strupr() for UPPeRcase and strlwr() for LoWeRcase conversion

mnemonics.title: CHARacter search
mnemonics.content: CHR - CHaRacter. Use strchr() to find first occurrence of a character in string

mnemonics.title: SUBString search
mnemonics.content: STR - SUBsTRing. Use strstr() to find first occurrence of substring in string

mnemonics.title: TOKENizer
mnemonics.content: TOK - TOKenize. Use strtok() to split string into tokens using delimiters

acronyms.usage: Use when you need to count number of characters in a string (excludes '\0')
Use when you need to copy one string into another variable
Use when you need to join two strings together (appends source to destination)
Use when you need to compare two strings lexicographically
Use when you need to reverse the order of characters in a string
Use when you need to find a specific character in a string
Use when you need to find if one string contains another string
Use when you need to split a string into tokens based on delimiters

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a security guard at the end of a string - everything after the '\0' is not part of the string. Visualize a string as a train with '\0' as the last car.
Think of copying files from one folder to another - the source stays, destination gets a copy. The destination must have enough space!
Imagine attaching two trains together - the second train (source) connects to the end of the first train (destination). Make sure destination has extra room!
Think of dictionary order - compare letter by letter. First difference decides winner. Visualize a balance scale weighing two words.
Think of searching for a specific book in a library row - you scan from left until you find it or reach the end.
Imagine cutting a bread loaf with knives at certain points - delimiters are the knife cuts, tokens are the bread pieces.

analogies.concept: String as an array of characters
analogies.analogy: A string is like a train with each character as a car. The engine is the first character, and the caboose is always the null character '\0' that signals the end.
Like photocopying a document - you have the original (source) and get a copy (destination). Both exist after the operation.
Like adding an extension to a building - the new part (source) gets attached to the existing building (destination), making it longer.
Like comparing two people by height - you measure both, compare each inch, and determine who is taller, shorter, or equal.
Like finding a specific student in a classroom by scanning the seating arrangement from the door until you spot them.
Like finding a specific phrase in a book - you search through pages until you locate where that sequence of words first appears.
Like cutting a sentence into individual words using spaces as scissors - each word becomes a separate token.
Like filling a parking lot - you must ensure your car (string data) fits in the parking space (destination array) or it will overflow to neighboring spots causing problems.
